methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $80M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Avalanche Energy is a Series A-stage climate and energy company founded by Robin Langtry that has raised $80M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, and Steady Energy at $110M) alongside a broader climate tech universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (LNZA).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a valuation proxy, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-subsequent-stage advancement, implying a valuation of $160M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $150M, with a reported range of $110M to $200M. Both methods cluster closely around the $150M–$160M band, suggesting consistent market pricing for this stage and sector.

Triangulation across the two methods produces a median valuation estimate of $155M, with a calculated range of $128M (low) to $184M (high). The tight alignment between stage-progression and peer-median results reduces model risk. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to peer companies, differentiation in core technology, and near-term funding runway—variables not fully specified in available data. The $155M median represents a reasonable central estimate absent material new information on burn rate or technical progress.
